methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Nyobolt, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Sai Shivareddy and Clare Grey, is assessed against a comp universe of 9 private and 1 public peer in the Climate & Energy sector. The peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), spanning valuations from $120M to $5.0B.

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. Stage-progression, applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to Nyobolt's $100M+ total raised proxy, implies $190M. Peer-median methodology applies the $355M median valuation across the 6-company same-stage, same-sector cohort. The peer range spans $120M (floor) to $5.0B (ceiling), reflecting meaningful dispersion in the market.

Triangulation across both methods yields a valuation corridor of $162M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $272M. This range reflects applied financial discipline rather than outlier-driven bounds. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity within the Climate & Energy sector—Commonwealth Fusion's $5.0B valuation significantly elevates the peer set and may reflect technology-stage or capital-intensity differentials not fully comparable to Nyobolt's current position. Stage-progression assumes standardized step-ups that may not account for company-specific risk or capital efficiency. The $100M+ total raised proxy warrants verification against actual deployment and burn metrics to validate the base anchor.
